keyTerms.East India Company: The British trading corporation that monopolized Asian trade, including tea, and effectively ruled large parts of India
keyTerms.Opium Wars: Two nineteenth-century conflicts in which Britain forced China to accept opium imports, funded by British tea consumption
keyTerms.Catherine of Braganza: The Portuguese wife of Charles II who popularized tea at the English court in the 1660s
keyTerms.Assam and Ceylon: British colonial territories in India and Sri Lanka where vast tea plantations were established using indentured labor
keyTerms.afternoon tea: The refined British ritual attributed to Anna, Duchess of Bedford, in the 1840s — a genteel surface over a violent imperial economy
